name: Comms Training
briefing: Welcome to Comms Training. Practice scanning frequencies, hailing contacts, and interpreting transmissions. Communications intelligence can reveal enemy intentions and unlock diplomatic options.

objectives.text: Review your communications panel and active channels
objectives.hint: The Comms panel shows detected signals, active hailing channels, and decoded transmissions. Your passive receivers scan automatically. Signals appear as frequency readouts on the spectrum display.

objectives.text: Tune your receiver to the contact's frequency
objectives.hint: Click on the detected signal or contact and select TUNE FREQUENCY. The receiver locks onto the signal. You may hear noise first — the signal becomes clearer as you fine-tune. Check the signal strength indicator.

With the frequency locked, press HAIL. This sends a standard identification request. The contact may respond, ignore, or become hostile. In a real mission, hailing before firing can prevent unnecessary conflict.

After hailing, listen for a response. Transmissions appear in the message log. If no response, the contact may be hostile, out of range, or using a different protocol. Report your findings to the Captain.
